required_inputs: request intent, requested action, patient/member context, service model, agreement or estimate posture, and intended output
home services operations boundary, adjacent specialist map, and tenant decision-rights matrix
clinical, payer, privacy, coding, agreement, pricing, technician, safety, legal, and approval indicators
refusal_triggers: request asks for diagnosis, treatment planning, plan-of-care change, clinical priority, medical necessity, payer interpretation, coding, claim disposition, PHI disclosure approval, pricing override, agreement exception, or licensed technician authority
request lacks required tenant facts, source records, authorization, referral, eligibility, order, agreement, consent, or technician readiness facts that would change disposition

required_inputs: CMS, eCFR, HHS OCR, AHRQ, ONC/ASTP, CMS quality, and state consumer-protection public sources
tenant source hierarchy, payer/provider manuals, agreement policy, service model, jurisdiction, and source freshness metadata
current patient/member, provider, payer, service date, service address, and system-of-record context
refusal_triggers: required public or tenant authority source is missing, stale, or materially contradicted
public guidance, payer rule, tenant policy, agreement term, clinical record, or jurisdictional requirement materially conflicts

required_inputs: EHR/EMR, referral, authorization, eligibility, order, plan-of-care, encounter, case-management, CRM, field-service, agreement, estimate, technician, charge, claim, denial, and appeal records
source timestamp, owner, retrieval hook, privacy posture, and tenant source-of-truth hierarchy
resolution, handoff, communication, and audit requirements
refusal_triggers: critical artifact is absent, stale, contradictory, not retrievable, or outside approved privacy handling
traceability from request to source evidence to action taken to owner to unresolved item cannot be established

required_inputs: systems-of-record map across EHR/EMR, clinical documentation, practice management, payer portal, revenue cycle, authorization, referral, eligibility, case management, CRM, field service, EVV or visit verification, and privacy/audit systems
tenant RACI, routing matrix, service model, agreement policy, escalation SLA, and prerequisite rules
authorization, referral, eligibility, order, plan-of-care, consent, service address, agreement, estimate, technician, appointment, equipment, and documentation dependencies
refusal_triggers: critical dependency owner is unresolved
systems of record materially disagree on patient/member, service date, authorization, referral, eligibility, order, service address, technician status, agreement state, or closure state

required_inputs: evidence completeness signals
authority completeness signals
source conflict indicators
patient-safety, PHI, payer, agreement, technician readiness, repeat-failure, and service-impact indicators
refusal_triggers: missing tenant facts or authority maps would change service disposition
clinical risk, privacy obligation, payer interpretation, agreement exception, safety-critical issue, owner conflict, or source conflict prevents safe synthesis

required_inputs: adjacent specialist map
authority boundary and handoff prerequisites
required evidence package for the adjacent owner
refusal_triggers: request belongs to clinical review, patient access, referral management, utilization management, medical coding, revenue cycle, privacy, compliance, payer operations, licensed technician support, or agreement/pricing approval
adjacent owner cannot be uniquely identified

required_inputs: in-scope home service request with tenant, patient/member, service date, service address, source records, agreement or estimate posture, authorization/referral/eligibility status, and technician readiness
documented service policy, resolution criteria, stakeholder communication rule, and closure evidence requirement
minimum-necessary privacy posture and allowed action boundary
refusal_triggers: resolution requires clinical, payer, privacy, pricing, agreement, technician, legal, safety, or approval authority outside the lane
required records or owner confirmations are missing

required_inputs: customer, patient/member, caregiver, internal stakeholder, branch, technician, case-management, or payer-adjacent communication context
approved message scope, PHI handling rule, source evidence, unresolved items, and next owner
tenant communication policy and consent or representative constraints
refusal_triggers: requested communication would disclose PHI beyond role, consent, minimum-necessary, or tenant policy
communication would promise service, pricing, coverage, authorization, clinical outcome, or technician arrival without supporting evidence

required_inputs: unsupported workaround, exception, repeat failure, conflicting source, or blocked service request
service impact, risk, authority boundary, missing prerequisite, owner route, and escalation SLA
tenant exception policy and evidence required for human review
refusal_triggers: exception would override policy, pricing, eligibility, agreement, authorization, referral, privacy, payer, clinical, or safety limits
repeat failure or conflicting evidence creates patient/member harm, privacy exposure, revenue leakage, or compliance risk

required_inputs: request content, patient-safety indicators, order, plan-of-care reference, technician note, and care-team authority map
administrative facts that can be summarized safely
clinical decision, licensed review, treatment, diagnosis, triage, discharge, or plan-of-care indicators
refusal_triggers: request asks for diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical priority, care-plan change, discharge readiness, medication, supply substitution requiring clinical judgment, or licensed review
patient safety indicator requires clinical manager or licensed clinician action

required_inputs: PHI sensitivity, consent or representative facts, minimum-necessary rule, role-based access, audit requirement, patient/member safety signal, and incident or complaint context
tenant privacy policy, clinical safety escalation path, and source-system links
communication recipient, distribution scope, service impact, and urgency
refusal_triggers: PHI disclosure, broad export, patient/member address/contact distribution, diagnosis sharing, or payer note disclosure lacks authorized purpose, recipient, consent, role, or minimum-necessary basis
patient/member harm risk, unsafe home visit condition, missed-visit pattern, or deterioration signal requires clinical, safety, or privacy owner escalation
